selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

response_raw: {"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"Dealing with fake content on social media is a complex challenge that requires a multi-faceted approach. Platforms must implement robust systems for detecting and removing misinformation, while also fostering user education and critical thinking skills. By combining advanced AI detection tools, transparent policies, and active community engagement, social media can become safer and more trustworthy spaces for users to share information and connect with one another.","reasoning_summary":"To effectively address fake content on social media, platforms should: 1) Develop AI algorithms to detect misleading or false information through pattern recognition in text, images, and videos. 2) Establish clear community guidelines that prohibit the spread of misinformation. 3) Provide users with tools to report suspicious content easily. 4) Promote digital literacy programs to help users identify fake news. 5) Collaborate with fact-checking organizations to verify claims quickly. These steps combined create a comprehensive defense against the proliferation of false information.","tone":"Thoughtful and solution-oriented","citations":["AI detection systems: https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.10814","Social media guidelines: https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/W19-1504","Digital literacy: https://www.pnas.org/content/117/11/5600"],"safety_notes":"Platforms must balance between preventing misinformation and maintaining free speech. AI systems should be continuously improved to reduce false positives, which could suppress legitimate content.","used_persona_fields":["mode","topic","persona"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Dealing with fake content on social media is a complex challenge that requires a multi-faceted approach. Platforms must implement robust systems for detecting and removing misinformation, while also fostering user education and critical thinking skills. By combining advanced AI detection tools, transparent policies, and active community engagement, social media can become safer and more trustworthy spaces for users to share information and connect with one another.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively address fake content on social media, platforms should: 1) Develop AI algorithms to detect misleading or false information through pattern recognition in text, images, and videos. 2) Establish clear community guidelines that prohibit the spread of misinformation. 3) Provide users with tools to report suspicious content easily. 4) Promote digital literacy programs to help users identify fake news. 5) Collaborate with fact-checking organizations to verify claims quickly. These steps combined create a comprehensive defense against the proliferation of false information.
response_parsed.safety_notes: Platforms must balance between preventing misinformation and maintaining free speech. AI systems should be continuously improved to reduce false positives, which could suppress legitimate content.
